keywords: lsd reshapes cortical dynamics through peak frequency shifts
topics.title: Psychedelic effects on brain dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how psychedelics such as psilocybin and LSD alter cortical organization, functional connectivity, and large-scale brain dynamics. It also includes analytic approaches used to characterize these changes, such as EEG microstate analysis, dynamic causal modeling, and neural avalanche measures.

topics.title: Ketamine Effects on Brain Connectivity and Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on ketamine administration and acute challenge studies examining altered perception, dose-dependent responses, and therapeutic effects. It also highlights brain network and connectome analyses of neural information dynamics, molecular connectivity, and receptor-linked connectivity changes in healthy adults and major depression.

keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound for Essential Tremor Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on focused ultrasound thalamotomy for essential tremor, especially Vim-targeted interventions and their clinical outcomes. It also highlights network-level functional mapping, connectivity fingerprints, and longitudinal structural and functional reorganization linked to durable improvement.

topics.title: Human Claustrum Structure and Function
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine the human claustrum across structural, functional, and developmental dimensions, including morphology, volume, microstructure, and connectivity. The abstracts also link claustrum features to visual responses, cognition, and clinical conditions such as early schizophrenia and trigeminal neuralgia.

topics.title: Locus Coeruleus Imaging Across Brain States
topics.description: This cluster centers on imaging and characterization of the locus coeruleus in humans, including measures of its integrity, contrast, activity, and neuromelanin signal. The abstracts link locus coeruleus features to sleep, anxiety-like behavior, cognition, and Alzheimer disease using advanced MRI approaches.

topics.title: Structural and Biomarker Changes in REM Sleep Disorder
topics.description: This cluster centers on isolated REM sleep behavior disorder and related phenoconversion risk, with emphasis on structural brain abnormalities and neurodegenerative markers. Studies highlight brainstem, gray matter, white matter, and glymphatic-related alterations alongside cholinergic and hypothalamic involvement.

topics.title: Deep Brain Stimulation Network Targeting
topics.description: These studies focus on deep brain stimulation in Parkinson disease and related disorders, emphasizing stimulation targets, network mechanisms, and outcome prediction. The cluster also highlights connectomic analysis, brain modeling, and personalized approaches to optimize DBS programming and response.

topics.title: Neural Connectivity and Hallucinations Across Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural mechanisms and connectivity alterations associated with auditory and visual hallucinations, including biomarker-oriented studies and network dysregulation. The abstracts span schizophrenia and Parkinson-related disorders, with emphasis on functional and thalamo-cortical abnormalities.

topics.title: Parkinsonian Motor and Neurodegenerative Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease, emphasizing motor symptoms such as freezing of gait and dyskinesia alongside degeneration in the substantia nigra, basal ganglia, and brainstem. It also includes biomarker and imaging approaches, especially iron accumulation, diffusion MRI, fMRI, and dynamic causal modelling.

keywords: normative modeling of reduced basal ganglia pvs
topics.title: Perivascular Spaces and Glymphatic Dysfunction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on perivascular space abnormalities, glymphatic dysfunction, and related biomarkers across neurological and psychiatric conditions. It also includes MRI- and normative-modeling approaches used to detect and characterize these changes.

topics.title: Brain Networks and Mechanisms in Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on how chronic pain and experimental pain are represented in the brain, including neural markers, network pathways, and morphometric differences. It also highlights fear-avoidance, catastrophizing, and other cognitive-affective factors that shape pain perception.

keywords: the impact of bioenergetic challenges on functional brain dynamics
topics.title: Bioenergetic Challenges and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how acute metabolic perturbations, especially glucose-related manipulations, alter brain metabolism and functional activity. It links neurometabolic changes with network reorganization, default mode network effects, and cognitive consequences.

keywords: audio visual reactivations facilitate musical skill retention
topics.title: Neural and cognitive effects of music training
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music training and participation relate to musical skill, memory, and synchronization in the brain. It also includes developmental and twin-study evidence on neural predisposition and neurostructural pathways linking music engagement with cognition.

topics.title: Music, Emotion, and Reward Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how music listening shapes emotion, reward, and related neural representations, including cortico-subcortical and functional connectivity mechanisms. It also includes work on decoding affective responses and physiological measures such as skin conductance during music-related experience.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Fatigue in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue and cognitive fatigue, especially in multiple sclerosis, and investigates their neural underpinnings using imaging and neuropsychological approaches. Themes include network localization of fatigue, iron deposition, glutamatergic change, and prefrontal and connectivity-related alterations.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Network Reorganization After Brain Tumor Surgery
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain tumors and their surgical treatment alter functional connectivity, language networks, and compensatory reorganization. It also addresses predictors of postoperative deficits and recovery, especially in glioma and related lesions.

topics.title: Focal Cortical Dysplasia and Epilepsy Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on focal cortical dysplasia and related epileptogenic malformations in epilepsy patients, with emphasis on MRI-based characterization and network-level abnormalities. It also includes studies of cortical asymmetry, thalamic connectivity, and functional network overlap in lesion detection and disease understanding.

keywords: early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
distinct effects of stroke lesion topography on multiple sensorimotor tracts
hypoperfusion loadings for outcome prediction in acute ischemic stroke
topics.title: Stroke Outcome Prediction and Recovery
topics.description: This cluster focuses on stroke-related recovery, clinical outcomes, and prediction of motor and cognitive outcomes after acute ischemic stroke. It emphasizes how lesion features, connectivity measures, and hypoperfusion patterns relate to functional recovery and stroke severity.

topics.title: Lesion Mapping of Post-Stroke Language Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on mapping focal brain lesions, disconnection, and network effects to explain aphasia and language impairments after stroke. It also emphasizes language lateralisation, hemispheric reorganisation, and predicting post-stroke language outcomes.

keywords: constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome
constrained dynamic functional connectivity predicts surgical outcome of temporal lobe epilepsy
the functional structural reorganization of connector hubs
topics.title: Network biomarkers in temporal lobe epilepsy
topics.description: This cluster centers on temporal lobe epilepsy, especially refractory and focal cases, with emphasis on network-level dysfunction and functional-structural reorganization. It also highlights biomarkers and predictive imaging measures for surgical outcome, including dynamic connectivity, connector hubs, and stereo EEG.

topics.title: Epilepsy Network Mapping and Surgical Localization
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying epileptogenic and seizure onset zones in epilepsy, especially pediatric and focal cases, using multimodal neuroimaging and electrophysiology. It emphasizes connectome-based analyses, dynamic brain connectivity, and source imaging approaches for surgical planning and outcome prediction.

topics.title: Long COVID Brain and Cognitive Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurological and cognitive sequelae of long covid, including cognitive fog, altered brain networks, and vascular or metabolic dysfunction. It also highlights biomarker approaches such as electroencephalography and neuroimaging to track these changes.

keywords: multimodal imaging signatures of cognitive decline
topics.title: Lewy Body Disease and Cognitive Decline
topics.description: This cluster centers on Lewy body disease, prodromal stages, and associated cognitive decline across mild cognitive impairment and dementia. It also highlights multimodal imaging, EEG, and neuromodulatory biomarkers used to characterize disease progression.

topics.title: Neuroimaging and Network Changes in Traumatic Brain Injury
topics.description: This cluster centers on traumatic brain injury and concussion, especially mild and chronic forms, across pediatric, military, and repeat-blast contexts. It emphasizes multimodal neuroimaging findings on microstructural change and altered cortical, default mode, and salience-limbic networks linked to symptoms and recovery.

keywords: neural correlates of food choice modulate
the dieter 's dilemma attentional focus on health
topics.title: Neural Bases of Food Choice and Memory
topics.description: This cluster examines how neural correlates, attention, and cognitive control shape food choice, taste memory, and responses to healthy versus unhealthy foods. It also includes work on distraction, food-related decision making, and functional connectivity in eating behavior.

keywords: intrinsic striatal functional brain connectivity signature of compulsivity
topics.title: OCD Circuitry and Compulsivity in Youth
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder, especially youth samples, with emphasis on compulsivity and treatment-resistant presentations. It highlights frontostriatal and striatal-thalamic network alterations, including functional connectivity signatures and higher-order connectivity changes.

topics.title: Neural signatures of tinnitus perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural correlates and brain differences associated with tinnitus, including chronic and somatic subtypes. It also highlights auditory and prefrontal involvement, neurotransmitter levels, and structural alterations linked to tinnitus severity and suppression.

keywords: functional connectivity correlates of observed in session rumination
self reference prompts increase negative thoughts
topics.title: Rumination, Thought Flow, and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on depressive rumination and related negative thought processes in depression and major depressive disorder. It also includes work linking observed in-session rumination and thought flow to functional connectivity changes and intervention-related measures.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging simultaneous integration of eeg
topics.title: Multimodal Neuroimaging and OP-MEG Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on simultaneous multimodal neuroimaging, especially the integration of EEG, fNIR, and MEG with optically pumped magnetometers and OP-MEG setups. It also includes supporting methods such as denoising, spatial neural modeling, and hemodynamic response analysis.

topics.title: Transcranial Ultrasound Neuromodulation in Human Brain
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial ultrasound stimulation and neuromodulation studies, often paired with fMRI or functional ultrasound to probe human brain circuits. The abstracts emphasize target-specific effects, rapid neuroplasticity, and network-level responses in regions such as the thalamus and cingulate circuitry.

topics.title: CBT and Neural Correlates in Anxiety Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on cognitive behavioral therapy and related interventions for OCD, panic disorder, and anxiety disorder. It also emphasizes neural and physiological measures such as brain activation, cortical hemodynamics, and resting state functional network connectivity used to assess treatment effects and emotional processing.

keywords: subcallosal cingulate functional connectivity link to remission after electroconvulsive therapy
graph metrics predict electroconvulsive therapy response
topics.title: Network Predictors of Electroconvulsive Therapy Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and treatment-resistant depression in relation to electroconvulsive therapy response and remission. The abstracts emphasize brain network features, including functional connectivity and network control theory, as predictors of clinical outcome.

topics.title: Sleep State Dynamics and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sleep and wakefulness are organized into stage-specific brain states, with emphasis on NREM sleep, arousal transitions, and dynamic functional connectivity. Many studies combine EEG-fMRI to examine spindles, K-complexes, and related brain-state changes during sleep.

keywords: nonlinear eeg complexity reveal sleep relate neural signatures
topics.title: Sleep, Aging, and Neural Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sleep-related brain activity and neural signatures relate to aging, memory, and sleep disorders. It also includes studies of EEG complexity, dynamic connectivity, and related physiological interactions during sleep.

keywords: uncertainty aware open source bayesian motor threshold estimation tool
tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation
tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation and cortical silent period duration
topics.title: Advanced TMS measurement and targeting
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for improving transcranial magnetic stimulation measurement, targeting, and parameter estimation. It includes multi-locus and paired-pulse TMS, motor hotspot localization, coil orientation effects, and machine-learning or Bayesian approaches for uncertainty-aware guidance.

keywords: rich club eigenvector centrality predicts differential treatment response in chronic low back pain
topics.title: Brain Connectivity in Chronic Pain and Migraine
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain functional connectivity and subcortical network alterations across chronic pain conditions, including fibromyalgia, migraine, and rheumatoid arthritis. It also includes predictive and computational approaches for treatment response and imaging-based quantification.

keywords: efficacy of personalized connectivity guide brain stimulation in treatment resistant depression
topics.title: Personalized Neuromodulation for Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on individualized noninvasive brain stimulation approaches for depression, especially treatment-resistant and related mood disorders. It emphasizes connectivity-guided targeting, stimulation efficacy, and neuroimaging-informed neuromodulation strategies.

keywords: causal mapping of transcranial electrical stimulation and craving reduction in addictive disorder
topics.title: tDCS and craving reduction in addiction
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial electrical stimulation for reducing craving and improving cognitive control in addictive disorder, especially alcohol use disorder. It also includes studies linking stimulation outcomes to brain regions and predictors such as prefrontal structure and insula-related control processes.

topics.title: Lifespan Brain Development and Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain structure and cognition change across the human lifespan, from neurodevelopmental trajectories to aging. It also includes population-specific and sex-related differences in brain charts and prediction of individual variation.

topics.title: Brain structure in diabetes and cognition
topics.description: This cluster focuses on structural brain changes associated with type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and glycemic state, especially gray matter loss and region-specific morphometric differences. It also links these brain characteristics to cognitive impairment and cognitive function in older adults over time.

keywords: local correlation of brain function in youth onset
topics.title: Brain and Metabolic Changes in Youth
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain connectivity, reward circuitry, and structural changes associated with obesity, anorexia nervosa, and youth-onset metabolic conditions. It also includes biomarkers and functional differences linked to development, treatment, and neurocognitive outcomes.

topics.title: Normative Modeling in Human Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative modeling approaches that define healthy reference distributions and map deviations in brain imaging data beyond group averages. It spans calibration, generalization, and atlas-building across MRI, DTI, EEG, and related neuroimaging phenotypes.

keywords: brain age gap reduction following exercise mirrors clinical improvements
topics.title: Brain Age Prediction and Accelerated Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on estimating brain age from MRI and related models, with emphasis on prediction performance, generalisation, and validation across populations. It also highlights accelerated brain aging, brain age gaps, and links to aging-related clinical and structural changes.

keywords: map resting state eeg microstate sources
topics.title: ALS Network Dynamics and Biomarker Discovery
topics.description: This cluster centers on amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, especially sensorimotor network reorganisation and directional alterations linked to EEG, MRI, and neuronal avalanche analyses. It also emphasizes biomarker exploration and machine learning approaches for characterizing disease progression and brain dynamics.

keywords: classification of white matter lesions in
topics.title: MRI Biomarkers in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based characterization of multiple sclerosis, especially myelin and structural brain changes across disease progression. It also includes cognitive status, lesion evolution, and biomarker-oriented multimodal imaging approaches.

keywords: federated neuroimaging data management platform for multisite studies
facilitate open and reproducible meta analysis with automation
topics.title: Federated Neuroimaging Workflows and Infrastructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, scalable neuroimaging infrastructure for shared data management, workflow execution, and tool integration across sites. It also emphasizes reproducible processing, BIDS-based standardization, and cloud-enabled collaborative platforms for clinical and research neuroimaging.

topics.title: Cross-Border Neuroimaging Policy and Open Science
topics.description: This cluster focuses on legal, ethical, and infrastructural issues surrounding cross-border neuroimaging data sharing, especially in the context of EU rules and open science. It also reflects broader global and Latin American perspectives on research capacity, governance, and the future of neuroimaging.

keywords: brain metabolic networks underlying integration and disease
gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
functional and metabolic network signatures of
topics.title: Brain Metabolic Network Signatures in Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on how metabolic and mitochondrial organization shapes cortical and brain network architecture, including gradients, connectivity, and inter-metabolite relationships. It also links these neurometabolic patterns to disease and cellular specialization.

keywords: elastic net multiple kernel learning models
topics.title: Foundation Models for Multimodal Brain Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain foundation models and related machine learning approaches for predicting clinical and phenotypic outcomes from neuroimaging and other sources. It emphasizes multimodal predictive modelling, external validation, and comparisons with classical models in psychiatry and memory-related studies.

keywords: structural mri volumetry improve differential diagnosis
topics.title: Frontotemporal Dementia, Social Cognition, and Diagnosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on behavioral and genetic frontotemporal dementia, with emphasis on social cognition, disinhibition, and criminal or norm-transgressive behavior. It also highlights structural MRI and related biomarkers used to improve differential diagnosis and characterize frontal-striatal degeneration.

topics.title: Exercise and brain connectivity across adulthood
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how physical exercise and intervention-based activity relate to changes in brain organization, connectivity, and white matter structure across the adult lifespan. It also links these neural changes to cognition, with emphasis on older and sedentary adults.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study
topics.title: Hormonal Modulation of Female Brain Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on how menstrual cycle, menopausal transition, and neuroendocrine factors shape brain activity, connectivity, and structure in females. It also includes studies linking hormone-related brain changes to epilepsy, emotion regulation, and cerebrovascular function.

keywords: menopause specific hippocampal subfield volumes and memory at midlife a period of change
topics.title: Midlife Menopause and Memory Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the menopause transition and midlife hormonal changes relate to hippocampal subfields, memory, and cognitive aging. It also includes associations with cognitive reserve and risk for neurobiological change.

topics.title: White Matter Hyperintensities and Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter hyperintensities, white matter integrity, and related small vessel disease changes across aging and neurodegenerative conditions. It also emphasizes their clinical correlates, cognitive effects, and longitudinal quantification in imaging studies.

keywords: abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Autism Development and Connectivity Alterations
topics.description: This cluster centers on autism and related developmental differences in childhood and youth, especially atypical brain organization and functional connectivity. It also includes EEG and functional neuroimaging findings that highlight altered neural dynamics and hierarchy development.

keywords: atlas guided region wise shapley value
topics.title: Deep Learning for Neurodegenerative MRI Classification
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based classification and prediction of neurodegenerative conditions, especially Alzheimer disease, dementia detection, and mild cognitive impairment. It emphasizes deep learning approaches, transfer learning, and explainable methods for robust clinical neuroimaging analysis.

topics.title: fMRI Dynamics and Network Coupling
topics.description: This cluster centers on fMRI signal dynamics, hemodynamic response modeling, and methods for detecting coupling and oscillatory activity in brain networks. It includes work on resting-state and task-related connectivity, with emphasis on fast and dynamic analyses.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity Mapping in MRI
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI approaches for measuring cerebrovascular reactivity, baseline cerebral blood flow, and related perfusion physiology. It includes ASL and BOLD-based methods, multi-delay acquisitions, and applications involving hypercapnia and resting-state cerebrovascular reactivity.

topics.title: NeuroAI and Brain State Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on NeuroAI approaches that relate neural representations to brain state dynamics, cognitive control, and task structure. It also includes multimodal fMRI-EEG methods, hidden Markov models, and validation of models across diverse tasks and stimuli.

topics.title: Early Stress, Resilience, and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how childhood trauma, maltreatment, and social stress relate to emotion regulation, memory suppression, and psychological resilience. It emphasizes neurobiological signatures and connectivity-based markers across transdiagnostic mental health studies.

keywords: bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
a tool for transparent and standardized assessment of multisite harmonization in neuroimaging
topics.title: Multisite Neuroimaging Harmonization Methods
topics.description: This cluster focuses on methods for correcting site, batch, and heterogeneity effects in multisite neuroimaging data. It includes harmonization frameworks, longitudinal MRI approaches, and tools for standardized assessment of harmonization efficacy.

keywords: brain state dynamics during emotional reflection
multimodal indicators of mood congruent emotional processing bias
topics.title: Neural and Mood Dynamics in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and major depressive disorder, with emphasis on mood-congruent emotional processing bias, emotional reflection, and associated brain state dynamics. It also includes multimodal measures linking ECG-fMRI signals, brain-heart interactions, and neural correlates of vulnerability and neurobiological scars.

keywords: genetic basis of human hippocampal organization genome wide analysis of multimodal imaging traits
intrinsic white matter functional activity bridges genes
topics.title: Genetic Architecture of Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how genetic and multi-omics analyses explain variation in human brain structure and organization, especially hippocampal and white matter traits. It also highlights shared heritability, latent brain subtypes, and integrative links between imaging measures and biological mechanisms.

keywords: cerebellar structural networks link dual physical cognitive impairment
topics.title: Aging, Cerebellum, and Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging relates to cognitive and motor changes, especially episodic memory, dual-task performance, and age-related decline. It emphasizes cerebellar structure and functional organization, white matter changes, and cortico-cerebellar pathways in older adults.

topics.title: Neuroimaging of Suicide Risk in Mood Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on suicide risk, suicidal ideation, and suicide attempts across depressive and bipolar disorders. It emphasizes multimodal brain imaging findings, including functional connectivity, hippocampal volumes, and network-level alterations linked to self-harm outcomes.

keywords: prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories of brain development
prenatal mental health early brain structure shape infant temperament
maternal distress × cortico limbic connectivity predicts
topics.title: Maternal Mood and Early Brain Development
topics.description: Studies in this cluster examine how prenatal and perinatal maternal mental health, exposures, and distress relate to infant and child brain development and later emotional outcomes. The abstracts emphasize longitudinal cohort evidence linking early environmental and maternal factors to brain structure, connectivity, and temperament.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment and Mental Health
topics.description: This cluster centers on the ABCD study and related longitudinal work examining how childhood and adolescence shape neurodevelopment, brain structure, and behavior. It highlights ADHD, anxiety and depression, environmental exposure, adversity, and resilience as linked influences on developmental outcomes.

topics.title: Adolescent Brain Connectivity and Substance Risk
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how adolescent brain development and functional connectome features relate to substance use risk, family history, and developmental trajectories. It includes studies of cannabis exposure, predicting later substance use, and identifying neuroimaging biomarkers of vulnerability.

keywords: functional neural predictors of early alcohol initiation in emerging adolescence
topics.title: Neural Predictors of Early Alcohol Use
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging and brain-circuit markers associated with alcohol use disorder, alcohol consumption, and early initiation of drinking. It emphasizes reward, inhibitory control, and corticostriatal/salience network measures as functional predictors in adolescence and related risk contexts.

keywords: functional connectivity predict crave across substance use disorder
ventral striatum connectivity predicts craving and abstinence
central neural actions of ibogaine in opioid use disorder
topics.title: Craving and Connectivity in Substance Use Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on craving, abstinence, and neural connectivity across substance use disorders, with emphasis on transdiagnostic circuit markers and prediction of outcomes. It also includes therapeutic and imaging studies involving ibogaine, neurofeedback, and personalized neuromodulation.

topics.title: PTSD Network and Biomarker Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on PTSD and related trauma populations, especially women and survivors, with emphasis on combat-related and complex PTSD. It highlights network-level brain alterations, structural-functional coupling, resting-state connectivity, and neurotransmitter-related biomarkers, including predictive signatures for early PTSD detection.

keywords: universal low cost open source device
topics.title: Multimodal Studies of Attention and Learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroscience studies of attention, associative learning, and autonomic or eye-blink responses, often using EEG, fMRI, and related measurement approaches. It also includes work on specialized recording devices and low-cost neuroimaging hardware.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
topics.title: Neural Dynamics in Dyscalculia Intervention
topics.description: This cluster centers on dyscalculia and related numerical learning difficulties, especially how intervention and training affect math gains and brain network reorganization. It emphasizes longitudinal changes in network dynamics, effective connectivity, and hypothesized neural mechanisms such as neural noise.

keywords: developmental brain network trajectories differentiate resilience
topics.title: Transdiagnostic Structural Vulnerability in Psychosis
topics.description: Studies of psychosis and schizophrenia across development highlight shared transdiagnostic structural brain patterns, especially cortical thickness alterations and vulnerability-related network trajectories. The cluster emphasizes how genetic and neuroimaging findings relate to resilience, symptom expression, and broader psychiatric disorder risk.

topics.title: Schizophrenia Subtypes and Network Alterations
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and the psychosis spectrum, emphasizing subtype differences, symptom phenotypes, and early psychosis. It also highlights altered brain connectivity, cerebellar-cortical circuitry, and treatment response differences across patients.

keywords: the future predict individual tau progression
topics.title: Disease progression and individualized neurodevelopmental risk
topics.description: The cluster centers on prediction and stratification of neurodegenerative and neurodevelopmental conditions, especially Alzheimer disease and multiple sclerosis. It emphasizes biomarker progression, individualized risk mapping, and heterogeneous pathways that shape cognitive phenotype and disease subtype.

topics.title: Naturalistic Brain Dynamics and Network Organization
topics.description: These studies examine how the brain responds to naturalistic stimuli and rest, focusing on neural representations, temporal dynamics, and network-level organization. They also address variability, reliability, and predictive structure in functional connectivity and brain-state dynamics.

topics.title: Inflammation and Connectivity in Depression
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how inflammatory processes relate to depression and major depressive disorder, including peripheral and neuroinflammatory markers. It also highlights associated brain connectivity and network changes, especially in amygdala-frontal and default mode network measures.

keywords: fmri base brain function symptom integration
topics.title: Brain Heterogeneity in Mood Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression, major depressive disorder, and bipolar disorder, with emphasis on heterogeneous brain signatures, clinically distinct subtypes, and symptom-brain relationships. It also includes large-scale ENIGMA studies using functional connectivity and machine learning to predict severity and classify disorder patterns.

keywords: high fidelity infant brain mri segmentation
topics.title: Pediatric and Neonatal Brain MRI Segmentation
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated segmentation and mapping of infant, neonatal, and pediatric brain MRI, including hippocampal and other subfield structures. It emphasizes robustness, longitudinal compatibility, and clinically acquired data across developmentally challenging scans.

topics.title: Multimodal Biomarkers for Alzheimer Detection
topics.description: This cluster focuses on multimodal biomarker and machine learning approaches for early Alzheimer diagnosis, prediction, and disease staging. It includes MRI, PET, plasma proteomic, and methylation-based methods aimed at distinguishing dementia syndromes and tracking neurodegenerative progression.

topics.title: Multimodal Alzheimer's Biomarker and Imaging Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer's disease across preclinical and prodromal stages, with emphasis on amyloid and tau PET, blood biomarkers, and disease heterogeneity. It also includes multimodal and deep learning approaches for linking imaging, functional connectivity, and subject-level prediction.

topics.title: Amyloid and Tau in Aging and Resilience
topics.description: This cluster focuses on Alzheimer-related pathology across the AD continuum, emphasizing amyloid and tau burden, neurodegenerative vulnerability, and links to cognition in cognitively normal older adults. It also highlights biological mechanisms of resilience and mismatch, including astroglial and blood-brain barrier-related processes.

topics.title: Neonatal Brain Injury and Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain development and structural alterations in vulnerable neonates, especially preterm infants and those with hypoxic ischaemic encephalopathy or congenital heart disease. Studies examine white matter, hippocampal, and thalamic changes using volumetric MRI and related imaging measures.

topics.title: Lifespan Development of Functional Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional brain organization changes across development and aging, with emphasis on individualized network mapping and variability. The abstracts highlight developmental trajectories, network reorganization, and maturation of functional connectivity across the human lifespan.

topics.title: Brain aging and connectivity across lifespan
topics.description: This cluster centers on how aging, lifespan, and sex-related differences shape brain networks and functional connectivity, often in relation to brain age prediction. The abstracts emphasize healthy and typical aging, maturation, and connectome-based relationships between structure and function.

keywords: maturation trajectories of functional brain network topology
topics.title: Early Brain Network Development
topics.description: Studies on developmental changes in neonatal and infant brain structure and functional network organization across early life. The cluster emphasizes maturation trajectories, functional parcellation, and longitudinal changes during critical early childhood periods.

topics.title: Preterm Birth and Neurodevelopmental Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on brain and behavioral development after preterm birth, with emphasis on cognitive and developmental outcomes in preterm infants and children. It also highlights connectivity and microstructural alterations as markers of altered neurodevelopment.

keywords: motor learning through imitation of fully visible
neural representation of perceived engagement during action observation
representational organization of hand actions across motor execution
topics.title: Action Observation and Motor Learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on how observing, imagining, and imitating hand actions supports grasping, motor execution, and short-term motor learning. It also highlights cortico-cerebellar control and neural representations during action observation and related behavioral effects.

keywords: cortical oscillations reflect adaptive updating of internal reach
bimanual vr mirror therapy modulates sensorimotor network activation after stroke
topics.title: Sensorimotor Mapping, Proprioception, and Body Ownership
topics.description: This cluster centers on how primary sensorimotor regions represent fingers, movement, and proprioceptive information, including changes in body maps and kinesthetic processing. It also includes studies of body ownership illusions and virtual-reality-based motor rehabilitation that modulate sensorimotor networks.

keywords: joint learning modulates brain synchrony in naturalistic fmri
the influence of visual perceptual load
topics.title: Learning, Development, and Brain Synchrony
topics.description: This cluster centers on how associative and perceptual learning relate to brain development, synchrony, and neural complexity. It also includes work on brain activity measures, predictive coding, and visual perceptual load in young adults.

keywords: map neural correlates of resting state
mouse rest state functional ultrasound imaging
functional mri compatible cortical wide field fluorescent
topics.title: Resting-State Brain Networks Across Imaging Modalities
topics.description: This cluster focuses on mapping resting-state brain activity and functional connectivity across human and mouse studies. It spans multiple imaging approaches, including fMRI, optical neuroimaging, calcium imaging, and functional ultrasound, to relate brain states and behavioral states to network dynamics.

keywords: protocol harmonized brain networks uncover key regions
distinct parkinson disease meg functional connectivity biomarker
topics.title: Brain Connectivity Biomarkers Across Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on multilayer and harmonized brain connectivity analyses for identifying patient-specific communities and biomarkers in neurodegenerative disease. It includes MEG-based studies, connectome fusion models, and work on Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, and lifespan-related network organization.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Language Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain supports language processing, including speech, reading, comprehension, and auditory functions. It also highlights bilingual and multilingual language organization, lateralization, and connectivity-related neural mechanisms.

keywords: distinct dynamic processing circuits ieeg reveal neural pathway specialization
long term abacus base mental calculation learning
topics.title: Neural and developmental bases of mathematical cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on how mathematical thinking is represented and processed in the brain, including digit processing, feedback, and specialized neural circuits. It also highlights developmental and experiential influences such as neurodevelopmental trajectories and abacus-based training.

topics.title: Reliability of fMRI Connectivity Measures
topics.description: This cluster centers on assessing the reliability and reproducibility of fMRI and resting-state connectivity metrics across repeated measurements. It also examines how factors such as scan length, cohort size, and denoising influence measurement validity and reliability.

keywords: benchmarke spatiotemporal fetal brain atlas construction
topics.title: Fetal Cortical Folding and Atlas Construction
topics.description: This cluster centers on fetal and developmental brain morphology, especially cortical folding, gyrification, and surface-based atlas construction. It also includes automated and open-source MRI pipelines for extracting and analyzing fetal brain surface features.

keywords: dorsal striatum activity reflect aging and inhibitory failure across the adult lifespan
topics.title: Aging, Inhibitory Control, and Brain Compensation
topics.description: This cluster centers on age-related changes in cognition and motor control across the adult lifespan, with emphasis on inhibitory failure, working memory control, and compensatory recruitment. It also includes network-level analyses linking dorsal striatum activity and broader brain systems to neurocognitive aging.

keywords: resting state eeg alpha bold coupling
topics.title: Connectomic Modeling of Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on modeling and inferring brain dynamics from connectomic and EEG-fMRI data, often using reduced biophysical and graph-based frameworks. It also emphasizes causal inference, multiscale organization, and how stimulation or resting-state signals shape functional integration and segregation.

keywords: eeg resting state complexity and entropy
topics.title: Thalamocortical Dynamics in Anesthesia and Consciousness
topics.description: This cluster centers on how propofol anesthesia alters consciousness through thalamocortical and network mechanisms, with emphasis on susceptibility, brain integrity, and EEG complexity measures. It also includes modeling and integrative analyses aimed at explaining individual differences and broader disorder-related effects.

topics.title: Age and Sex Effects in Brain Signals
topics.description: This cluster centers on lifespan and age related changes in brain anatomy and electrophysiology, including EEG and MEG measures across different populations. It also includes methodological approaches for handling neural feature space and spectral or tensor-based analysis.

keywords: evolution of the auditory cortex duplication of heschl 's gyrus in great apes
intentional communication orofacial premotor cortex shape
topics.title: Evolution of Primate Auditory and Language Cortex
topics.description: This cluster focuses on comparative neuroscience of primates, especially the evolution of auditory and language-related cortical regions in humans, great apes, and macaques. It emphasizes anatomical and functional changes underlying cortical duplication, communication, and evolutionary plasticity.

topics.title: Neural Representation and Universal Brain Encoding
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neural representations align across brains, models, and tasks, especially in human fMRI and decoding settings. It also emphasizes semantic and acoustic structure in naturalistic stimuli and the search for universal encoding principles.

topics.title: Superficial White Matter and Cortical Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on the organization of superficial white matter and related fiber pathways in human and comparative brain networks. It emphasizes structural connectomes, sulcal topology, and short- versus long-range connectivity patterns.

keywords: unsupervised modeling of eeg state space dynamics reveal latent neural profiles relate
topics.title: EEG Dynamics and Latent Neural States
topics.description: This cluster focuses on EEG-based characterization of dynamic brain states, including alpha-band modulation, resting-state markers, and latent neural profiles derived through unsupervised modeling. The studies relate these neural patterns to cognition, attention, working memory, and task or narrative conditions.

keywords: task evoked tonic aperiodic eeg activity
topics.title: Aperiodic EEG Signatures of Cognition and Traits
topics.description: This cluster centers on aperiodic EEG and related oscillatory features as markers of cognition, psychopathology, and stable individual differences. It also includes work on rest-state and task-evoked dynamics, alpha frequency measures, and neural fingerprinting.

topics.title: White Matter Mapping and Segmentation Across Age
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated analysis of brain MRI, especially segmentation and diffusion-based mapping of white matter structure. It also includes normative and age-related studies spanning infant to developmental cohorts, with attention to reproducibility and atlas construction.

topics.title: Multi-echo fMRI denoising and evaluation
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods for denoising and preprocessing multi-echo fMRI, especially ICA-based approaches and tools such as tedana. It also includes assessment metrics and reliability analyses for evaluating denoising performance and signal quality.

keywords: accelerate zero echo time functional mri
topics.title: Ultra-High-Field Functional Brain Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on ultra-high-field MRI approaches for mapping human brain function at very high spatial resolution. It emphasizes high-resolution fMRI, 7T imaging, and related mapping methods for studying cortical and deep brain organization.

topics.title: Mindfulness and Interoception in Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on mindfulness practices and their links to interoceptive processing, bodily representation, and gut-brain interactions. It also includes fMRI-based analyses of functional brain networks, regional activation, and connectivity biomarkers.

topics.title: Prefrontal and Insular Connectivity Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on functional and structural connectivity patterns in the human prefrontal cortex, anterior cingulate, and insula. It also includes work on connectivity profile reliability, cortical gradients, and related organizational features.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on how emotion regulation is represented in the brain, especially during cognitive reappraisal, emotional interference, and amygdala-related control. It also includes developmental, state-trait, and psychopathology-linked differences in regulation capability and modulation.

topics.title: Robust Brain Dynamics in Child Cognition
topics.description: The cluster centers on brain-behavior relationships and individualized functional network dynamics, with emphasis on robust network recovery and topology in child and general cognition studies. It also includes meta-analytic and robustness-oriented approaches for interpreting limited temporal fMRI data.

keywords: multimodal brain connectivity integration for cognitive performance prediction in youth
comparative evaluation of latent modeling approaches
topics.title: Multimodal Brain Connectivity for Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on predicting behavior and cognition from multimodal brain connectivity, especially in youth. It emphasizes individualized network models, functional connectomes, and comparative evaluation of latent modeling approaches for brain-behavior prediction.

keywords: the neural underpinnings of social threat
the functional heterogeneity of the brain during threat processing
topics.title: Fear, Threat, and Anxiety Circuits
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural and autonomic basis of fear conditioning, extinction, and threat processing in anxiety. It emphasizes amygdala-centered circuitry, social threat, and differences in brain responses associated with trait and social anxiety.

keywords: spatiotemporal dynamics of visual mental imagery of simple and complex content
cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
topics.title: Neural Basis of Visual Mental Imagery
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how visual mental imagery is represented in the brain, including vividness, aphantasia, and individual variation in imagery experience. It emphasizes reactivation of early visual cortex and feedback-related spatiotemporal dynamics across different content types.

topics.title: Human Connectome Structure and Hierarchy
topics.description: This cluster focuses on reconstructing and modeling human brain connectivity, with emphasis on directed structural networks, geometry, and diffusion-based dynamics. It also examines hierarchical organization, sensory integration, and relationships between anatomical structure and functional profiles across cortex.

topics.title: Human Brain Connectivity and Effective Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional and effective connectivity in the human brain are measured and compared across imaging modalities. It emphasizes dynamic network organization, thalamic and structure-function relationships, and links between connectivity patterns and cognitive functioning.

keywords: decode voice identity in auditory working memory
topics.title: Hippocampal and Working Memory Neural Correlates
topics.description: This cluster centers on how hippocampal and cortical systems support working memory, episodic retrieval, and memory representation. It also includes neural dynamics and fMRI-based evidence for flexible storage, mismatch detection, and content-specific decoding in auditory and episodic memory tasks.

keywords: neural networks of prefrontal and hippocampal contributions to conceptual change
topics.title: Neural Bases of Conceptual and Attentional Change
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how prior knowledge, prefrontal and hippocampal systems, and broader control networks support conceptual change, rule updating, and science learning. It also includes work on selective attention, flexible attentional switching, and the neural dynamics underlying intuitive and semantic decision processes.

keywords: brain dynamics underlying value base decision making
neural correlates of optimal risk taking
topics.title: Neural and Computational Basis of Risky Choice
topics.description: This cluster centers on how reward learning, value computation, and prediction error signals shape risky decisions and optimal risk taking. It also includes computational and neural approaches examining choice history, exploration-exploitation, and habitual behavior.

keywords: fnirs evidence for consistent affective representations across viewing and recall
topics.title: Affective Memory and Face Perception
topics.description: These studies examine how affective representations are maintained across viewing and recall, with links to neural reinstatement, behavioral response, and face processing. The cluster also includes work on misinformation, uncertainty, and individual predictors in memory-related neuroscience.

keywords: early neural response to emotional speech
topics.title: Neural Processing of Emotional Cues
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain processes emotional information, especially speech and facial cues, across dimensions such as valence, arousal, and bias. It highlights amygdala involvement, early neural responses, and ERP-related evidence in emotion perception and prediction error detection.
